nodes.name: 14th Lowland
nodes.excerpt: The 14th Lowland is an infantry regiment known for labor and construction duties.
nodes.notes: The 14th Lowland is a provincial infantry regiment where Dellan Moor served for four years. The regiment was primarily used for labor and construction tasks. Sable Voss was a supply administrator in this regiment. Dellan deserted from this regiment fourteen months ago.

nodes.name: Admiralty
nodes.excerpt: The organization Maren Ashveil worked for as a surveyor.
nodes.notes: The Admiralty is the organization Maren Ashveil worked for as a licensed surveyor for a decade before resigning under undisclosed circumstances. An incident during a mapping expedition led to her voluntary withdrawal.

nodes.name: Akaashi
nodes.excerpt: Akaashi is a customer at the coffeeshop who is a photography student.
nodes.notes:
Akaashi is a quiet and reserved photography student who frequents the coffeeshop where Bokuto works. He is described as beautiful and carries a camera with him, indicating his passion for photography. Akaashi is initially indifferent to Bokuto's flirtations but eventually offers him a modeling job for a photography project. Over time, Akaashi and Bokuto develop a deeper connection, with Akaashi showing a dry sense of humor and a gentle demeanor. He is focused on his work and values professionalism, as seen when he insists on waiting until the project is over before pursuing a personal relationship with Bokuto.

Akaashi Keiji is introduced as Kageyama's father, a supportive and caring parent who shares his son's interest in volleyball. He is depicted as a handsome and composed individual, catching Bokuto's attention and sparking a romantic interest. Akaashi's interactions with Bokuto are polite and respectful, and he is shown to be a skilled volleyball player himself, participating in games with the children. His character adds depth to the narrative, bridging the gap between the children's world and the adult responsibilities of parenthood.

nodes.name: Aldric Senn
nodes.excerpt: Aldric Senn ran the primary gray-market cargo operation through Tiveck.
nodes.notes: Aldric Senn maintained the Sunken Ledger and managed the gray-market cargo operation in Tiveck for thirty years. He died of natural causes eleven years ago, leading to the fragmentation of his operation.

nodes.name: Asas São Paulo
nodes.excerpt: Asas São Paulo is a professional team in the Brazil Super League.

nodes.name: Bokuto
nodes.excerpt: Bokuto is a barista at a coffeeshop who becomes infatuated with a customer named Akaashi.
nodes.notes:
Bokuto is a lively and charismatic barista working at a coffeeshop called Nekoma's. He is immediately taken by Akaashi, a customer who visits the shop, and makes a bold attempt to flirt with him by writing his phone number on a cup. Despite Akaashi's initial lack of response, Bokuto remains hopeful and continues to be intrigued by Akaashi's quiet demeanor and passion for photography. Throughout the story, Bokuto's interactions with Akaashi evolve from awkward flirtations to a genuine connection, as they work together on Akaashi's photography project. Bokuto is characterized by his enthusiasm, humor, and a tendency to act impulsively, especially when it comes to matters of the heart.

Bokuto is a professional volleyball player who is deeply involved in his son Shouyou's life. He is depicted as a caring and attentive father, willing to go the extra mile to ensure his son's happiness and well-being. Bokuto is also shown to have a humorous and light-hearted personality, often engaging in playful banter with his son. His interactions with Kageyama's father, Akaashi Keiji, reveal a budding interest and attraction, adding a layer of complexity to his character. Bokuto's passion for volleyball is evident, and he takes pride in sharing this passion with the younger generation, including Kageyama Tobio.

nodes.name: Boris Hulwich
nodes.excerpt: Boris Hulwich is a weathered wanderer shaped by loss and exile.
nodes.notes: Boris Hulwich, once the son of a minor noble house on the fringes of Veldmoor, lost his family during a brutal border conflict that erased his lineage from official record. Stripped of title and inheritance, he became a wanderer—part mercenary, part reluctant guardian—moving through the marshlands and ruined keeps of the region. Known for his imposing build, ash-gray eyes, and calm voice, Boris carries himself with restraint, hiding a constant tension between vengeance and hope. His primary motive is survival with purpose, often drawn into conflicts due to a moral code he cannot escape. His closest bond is with Sable Voss, characterized by mutual reliance and a complex dynamic.

nodes.name: Coach Ukai
nodes.excerpt: Coach Ukai was wrapping himself up in warm scarves.

nodes.name: Daichi
nodes.excerpt: Daichi just looked at him knowingly.
nodes.notes: Daichi is a character who shares a knowing camaraderie with Sugawara. He is a team member who understands the dynamics between Hinata and Kageyama and often steps in to mediate when tensions rise. Daichi's presence is calming, and he is seen as a leader who helps maintain harmony within the team.

nodes.name: Deepwater Guild
nodes.excerpt: A loose network of small-cargo operators and traders at the eastern quay.
nodes.notes: The Deepwater Guild is an informal network of small-cargo operators, independent vessel owners, and harbor-adjacent traders working the eastern quay. It operates in the gray area between legality and prohibition, with no formal leadership or membership list. Fen Caldra is a central figure due to his long-standing presence and trust among members. The Guild uses the Salt Warren for storage, with Fen holding the only key. Orris Vane, the harbor master, tolerates their operations to avoid drawing attention. The Guild reflects Tiveck's blend of formal and informal economies, with most members having deep roots in the city.

nodes.name: Dellan Moor
nodes.excerpt: Dellan Moor is a former soldier living in hiding after deserting his regiment.
nodes.notes: Dellan Moor, 29, is a former soldier who served in the 14th Lowland infantry regiment. He deserted fourteen months ago during a reassignment to a punitive posting. Currently, he lives with Sable Voss in a disused highwayman's waystation on the moorland north of the main coastal road. They have been there for eight months, growing food and making occasional trips to rural markets. Dellan is skilled in carpentry, basic medicine, and animal husbandry due to his regiment's focus on labor and construction. He trusts Sable completely, though he is uncertain about the permanence of their relationship. Dellan is lean, with a darker complexion and a regimental tattoo on his left shoulder.

nodes.name: Fen Caldra
nodes.excerpt: A careful and discreet operator of a private transport service.
nodes.notes: Fen Caldra is in his mid-40s, born in Tiveck, and has spent his life near the waterfront. He operates a small private transport service with two vessels and a trusted crew. Known for reliability and discretion, he uses the Salt Warren for gray-market storage. He discovered the Sunken Ledger six years ago and has kept it hidden. Fen has leverage over Orris Vane through a forged customs document and finds Maren Ashveil intriguing, suspecting she seeks the Ledger.

nodes.name: Iwaizumi
nodes.excerpt: Iwaizumi scowled at him.

nodes.name: Japan Men's National Volleyball Team
nodes.excerpt: Japan Men's National Volleyball Team participated in the 2021 Olympics.

nodes.name: Kageyama
nodes.excerpt: Kageyama ate politely and quietly.
nodes.notes:
Kageyama is a character who maintains a polite demeanor while eating but is quick to scold Hinata for his antics. He has a complex relationship with Hinata, characterized by both frustration and care. Kageyama is often seen taking care of Hinata, such as wiping his face or giving him his jacket when he's cold. Their relationship is central to the narrative, showcasing a blend of rivalry and affection.

Kageyama is portrayed as a serious and intense young boy with a passion for volleyball. He is a setter and aspires to be the best, showing a competitive spirit even at a young age. His relationship with Shouyou is complex; while he often comes across as grumpy and critical, there is an underlying respect and potential for friendship. Kageyama's interactions with Bokuto reveal his admiration for the sport and his desire to learn from experienced players. His father, Akaashi Keiji, plays a significant role in his life, supporting his volleyball interests.

nodes.name: Karasuno High
nodes.excerpt: Karasuno High is the school where Hinata and his teammates play volleyball.
nodes.notes: Karasuno High is a prominent setting in the Haikyū!! series, known for its Boys' Volleyball Team. The school was once famous for its volleyball team, particularly during the era of the 'Small Giant.' However, by the time Hinata joins, the team is struggling to regain its former glory. Under the guidance of Coach Ukai and the leadership of the third-year students, Karasuno aims to return to the national stage. The team is characterized by its determination and the unique quick attack combination of Hinata and Kageyama. Karasuno's rivalry with Nekoma High is a central theme, as both schools strive to meet each other at the national level.

nodes.name: Kenma
nodes.excerpt: Kenma is Kuroo's boyfriend, mentioned in the context of their relationship.

nodes.name: Kuroo
nodes.excerpt: Kuroo is Bokuto's coworker and friend at the coffeeshop.
nodes.notes: Kuroo works alongside Bokuto at the coffeeshop and is one of his close friends. He is characterized by his playful and teasing nature, often making jokes at Bokuto's expense, especially regarding his crush on Akaashi. Kuroo is supportive, though, and encourages Bokuto to pursue his interest in Akaashi. He is in a relationship with Kenma, and their dynamic is a source of amusement and warmth in the story.

nodes.name: Lev
nodes.excerpt: Lev is a coworker of Bokuto and Kuroo at the coffeeshop.
nodes.notes: Lev is another barista at the coffeeshop where Bokuto and Kuroo work. He is depicted as tall and somewhat laid-back, often seen lounging behind the cash register. Lev enjoys teasing Bokuto about his crush on Akaashi but is ultimately supportive of his friend. He has a cheerful disposition and is known for his loud reactions, particularly when startled or amused.

nodes.name: Maren Ashveil
nodes.excerpt: A client of Fen Caldra who is difficult to read and possibly seeking the Sunken Ledger.
nodes.notes: Maren Ashveil has been transported by Fen Caldra twice and has had two small errands done for her by him. Fen finds her interesting and suspects she is looking for the Sunken Ledger, though he has not disclosed its existence to her.

nodes.name: MSBY Black Jackals
nodes.excerpt: MSBY Black Jackals is a Division 1 team in the V-League.

nodes.name: Natsu Hinata
nodes.excerpt: Natsu Hinata is Shōyō Hinata's younger sister.

nodes.name: Nekoma's
nodes.excerpt: Nekoma's is the coffeeshop where Bokuto, Kuroo, and Lev work.

nodes.name: Nishinoya
nodes.excerpt: Hinata and Kageyama sitting together, just behind Tanaka and Nishinoya.

nodes.name: Oikawa
nodes.excerpt: Oikawa wanted to hit something.
nodes.notes: Oikawa is a character who experiences frustration after losing a match to Karasuno. Despite his competitive nature, he acknowledges the fairness of the game. Oikawa is intrigued by the close relationship between Kageyama and Hinata, feeling a mix of envy and amusement. His interactions with Iwaizumi reveal a playful dynamic.

nodes.name: Orrus Vane
nodes.excerpt: A person with whom Fen Caldra has a complex, functional arrangement.
nodes.notes: Orris Vane is implicated in a cargo irregularity due to a forged customs document commissioned by Fen Caldra. This leverage has kept both out of serious trouble, though it is not a friendship.

nodes.name: Sable Voss
nodes.excerpt: Sable Voss is Dellan Moor's companion and former supply administrator in the 14th Lowland regiment.
nodes.notes:
Sable Voss was a supply administrator in the 14th Lowland regiment. She and Dellan Moor made the decision to desert together after weeks of careful discussion. They have been living together in a waystation on the moorland for eight months. Sable is trusted completely by Dellan, though the nature of their relationship remains uncertain.

Sable Voss has a complicated bond with Boris Hulwich, characterized by mutual reliance rather than trust. She challenges Boris's rigid worldview, often pushing him toward risks he would otherwise avoid. Their relationship blurs the line between friendship and necessity, enduring because it is tested.

Sable Voss shares a complex bond with Boris Hulwich, characterized by mutual reliance rather than trust. She challenges Boris's rigid worldview, often pushing him toward risks he would otherwise avoid. Their relationship blurs the line between friendship and necessity, enduring through tests rather than simplicity.

nodes.name: Sable Voss's Account
nodes.excerpt: A draft account of supply falsification in the 14th Lowland Regiment.
nodes.notes: Sable Voss is drafting an account of the supply falsification she witnessed over six years, including dates, quantities, names, and the paper trail as she remembers it. She keeps it separate from other papers, in a sealed bundle under a flagstone.

nodes.name: Salt Warren
nodes.excerpt: A network of tidal caves and chambers used for gray-market storage.
nodes.notes: The Salt Warren is a network of tidal caves and excavated chambers beneath the eastern cliff face of Tiveck. It has been used for gray-market storage for generations. Fen Caldra currently uses it to store items for clients who prefer discretion.

nodes.name: Shōyō Hinata
nodes.excerpt: Shōyō Hinata is the main protagonist of the Haikyū!! series.
nodes.notes:
Shōyō Hinata is a passionate and energetic volleyball player who serves as the main protagonist of the Haikyū!! series. Initially a first-year student at Karasuno High, he plays as a middle blocker for the Boys' Volleyball Team. Despite his short stature, Hinata is known for his incredible jumping ability and tenacity on the court. Inspired by the 'Small Giant,' a former ace of Karasuno, Hinata is determined to become a formidable player despite his height. Over the series, he evolves from a nervous and inexperienced player into a key member of his team, known for his quick attacks and ability to motivate those around him. His journey takes him from Karasuno High to playing for the MSBY Black Jackals in the V-League and eventually joining the Japan Men's National Volleyball Team during the 2021 Olympics. As of 2022, Hinata is a member of Asas São Paulo in the Brazil Super League.

Hinata is a lively and energetic character, often seen chattering and making gestures while eating. He has a close relationship with Kageyama, often seen interacting closely with him. Despite being scolded by Kageyama, Hinata seems to enjoy his company and reciprocates his gestures of care.

Shouyou is a young, energetic boy who looks up to his father, Bokuto, and shares his enthusiasm for volleyball. He is depicted as having a crush on his classmate Kageyama, despite Kageyama's often grumpy demeanor. Shouyou's interactions with Kageyama are a mix of admiration and frustration, as he struggles to win over Kageyama's friendship. His character is marked by a sense of determination and a desire to improve, both in his social interactions and in his volleyball skills.

nodes.name: Small Giant
nodes.excerpt: The Small Giant was a former ace of Karasuno High.

nodes.name: Sugawara
nodes.excerpt: Sugawara watched his sleeping kouhais with pride.
nodes.notes: Sugawara is portrayed as a nurturing figure, watching over his younger teammates with a sense of pride. He enjoys the tranquility of bus rides and feels a paternal connection to his teammates, often referred to as 'his kids.' Sugawara captures moments of camaraderie and affection among the team, such as taking a photo of Hinata and Kageyama sleeping together.

nodes.name: Sunken Ledger
nodes.excerpt: The Sunken Ledger is a valuable and dangerous artifact hidden by Fen Caldra.
nodes.notes: The Sunken Ledger is an artifact that Fen Caldra found six years ago in a sealed crate within the Salt Warren. He read enough of it to understand its value and danger. Fen has kept it hidden in a location only he knows, contemplating its fate for years without deciding.

nodes.name: Supply Falsification Order
nodes.excerpt: A written order to falsify supply records in the 14th Lowland Regiment.
nodes.notes: This order was given to Sable Voss to obscure a supply shortage that would have embarrassed the regiment's commanding officer. It was the first time she was directly ordered to falsify records in writing.

nodes.name: Tanaka
nodes.excerpt: Hinata and Kageyama sitting together, just behind Tanaka.

nodes.name: The Wrecked Compass
nodes.excerpt: A dockside tavern on the eastern quay known for its weathered compass rose.
nodes.notes: The Wrecked Compass is a dockside tavern located on the eastern quay, occupying the ground floor of a narrow stone building. It is named after a compass rose painted on its exterior wall, now weathered and illegible. The tavern is known for its low ceilings, warm atmosphere, and the smell of salt and woodsmoke. It serves primarily dock workers, small-cargo operators, and travelers who avoid larger taverns frequented by Admiralty personnel. Regulars are loyal and discreet, and new faces are noted. Fen Caldra is a frequent visitor, and the Deepwater Guild meets here informally. Maren Ashveil has visited twice to meet Fen. The tavern has a back room available for private hire.

nodes.name: Tiveck
nodes.excerpt: A harbor town with shifting sandbar patterns.
nodes.notes: Tiveck is a harbor town where Maren Ashveil arrived three weeks ago to document the harbor's shifting sandbar patterns for a merchant consortium. It is also where she is secretly searching for the Sunken Ledger.

nodes.name: Tsukishima
nodes.excerpt: Tsukishima would rather not sit here.
nodes.notes: Tsukishima is a character who prefers to sit with people he knows rather than strangers, even if he finds them annoying. He observes the habits of his friends, Hinata and Kageyama, with a mix of amusement and disdain. Despite his sarcastic remarks, he seems to have a level of understanding and familiarity with them.

nodes.name: Virsa Col
nodes.excerpt: Virsa Col is a retired records archivist with a secretive past.
nodes.notes: Virsa Col, in her late 50s, worked as a part-time records archivist at the Admiralty's records depot in Tiveck for eleven years before retiring. She is known for her preference for quiet and meticulous record-keeping. Sixteen years ago, she misfiled cargo inspection reports at the request of Orris Vane, which she has since regretted. Maren Ashveil is close to discovering her involvement. Virsa lives alone, enjoys gardening, and reads extensively. She is quiet, careful, and has a strong sense of morality, though she struggles with past decisions.

nodes.name: Warden's Seal
nodes.excerpt: A forged customs clearance document with strategic leverage.
nodes.notes: The Warden's Seal is a forged customs clearance document in official Admiralty format, bearing the counter-seal of the Port Warden's office. It certifies the inspection and clearance of a cargo shipment that was never inspected, with Orris Vane's administrative identifier inserted by the forger. Fen Caldra commissioned it twelve years ago to consolidate his position in Tiveck's gray market. Although he has never used it actively, the knowledge of its existence ensures Orris Vane's cooperation with Fen's harbor operations. Fen keeps it in a strongbox at a lodging house in Tiveck, under a false name. The document ties Orris to a specific shipment, which could lead to further connections if investigated. Maren Ashveil, with her Admiralty background, might recognize its significance. The Seal and the Sunken Ledger together could expose a network if Maren and Fen decide to collaborate.

nodes.name: Waystation on the Moorland
nodes.excerpt: A disused highwayman's waystation where Dellan Moor and Sable Voss live.
nodes.notes: The waystation on the moorland is a disused highwayman's station located north of the main coastal road. It is approximately two days' walk from the nearest town of any size. Dellan Moor and Sable Voss have been living there for eight months, utilizing its mostly intact roof, fireplace, and working well. They have stopped moving because the waystation is sufficient for their needs.

nodes.name: Yamaguchi
nodes.excerpt: Even Yamaguchi thought the same.

links.label: knows concealed entrance to Salt Warren
